title: Is American Tower A Buy After The Much Needed Correction?
text: With the tightening capital market and dilutive/debt-laden nature of REITs in general, it is unsurprising that AMT has similarly lost -43% of its value since the August 2021 top. For example, AMT still reports elevated long-term debts of $35.58B, with the expanding weighted average interest rate contributing to the increase in its annualized interest expenses to $1.39B. Then again, the management has moderately raised its FY2023 property revenue guidance to $10.88B (+3.9% YoY), adj EBITDA to $6.99B (+6.5% YoY), and AFFO per share to $9.70 (inline YoY).

title: This REIT Myth MUST Be Debunked
text: The markets reacted negatively to the Federal Reserve's comments on interest rates, causing declines in major stock gauges. The belief that rising interest rates negatively impact REITs is a myth, as REITs offer growth potential and higher total returns compared to bonds. REITs like American Tower, VICI Properties, and Extra Space are attractive investment opportunities with strong fundamentals and potential for future growth.

title: Lesson Learned: Stay In Your Circle Of Competence

The concept is likened to a baseball analogy, where waiting for the right pitch in the right location leads to success. We share personal examples of straying outside of a circle of competence, resulting in financial losses. And we share some ideas on a few REITs as well.

title: American Tower Corporation Prices Senior Notes Offering
text: BOSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--American Tower Corporation (NYSE: AMT) today announced the pricing of its registered public offering of senior unsecured notes due 2028 and 2033 in aggregate principal amounts of $750.0 million and $750.0 million, respectively. The 2028 notes will have an interest rate of 5.800% per annum and are being issued at a price equal to 99.828% of their face value.
